metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4830
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 7: Protein 4.1B (EPB41L3) enhances synaptic localization of NMDARs in cultured hippocampal neurons.

surface-expressed NMDARs were labeled with GFP-tagged NR2B. shRNA knockdown of protein 4.1B (EPB41L3) reduced surface NR2B and overexpression of 4.1B resulted in enhanced localization of GFP-NR2B at synapses. This effect was specific to the FERM domain of 4.1B.

Authors suggest that "protein 4.1B plays an important role in the delivery of NMDAR subunits to synapses in hippocampal neurons, and that the FERM domain through which it may interact with SynCAM1 is necessary for this function.". Experimental description: M&M: "Medium density hippocampal cultures were prepared from embryonic day 19 Sprague Dawley rat pups as described (Brewer et al., 1993), with minimal modifications"

uniprot accession for rat species selected for this annotation was closest to the reviewed mouse uniprot accession Q9WV92.

antibody / targeting:
"To generate shRNA to mouse 4.1B, the following sequence of 4.1B was subcloned into the pSuper vector (Brummelkamp et al., 2002): 5'- CGTGACCGGCTTCGAATAA -3."

Nucleotide BLAST top-hits are all from gene EPB41L3, e.g. NM_001395724.
